description:
# Open question

`bl new` (create) walkthrough end-to-end — focus on plugin-assigned ball ID.

Settled model (per §7 worktree-first, NO return channel — supersedes this topic's original `field_overrides` framing):
- balls stages `tasks/<provisional-id>.md` in the `changes/<uuid>/` worktree (uuid-named, so nothing depends on the id up front).
- A `new/pre/` plugin that wants to assign an id (e.g. jira → `PROJ-1234`) does `git mv` + a frontmatter edit IN THE WORKTREE; it returns nothing. balls re-reads the tree after the pre-chain to learn the final id.
- If no plugin claims the id, balls finalizes it from `id_scheme` (bl-f3e0).
- Commit carries the final id in the §5 trailer block (`bl-id: <final>`); `new` has no `bl-from/to-state`.

Spec the deterministic sequence and the failure-mode list:
- Two plugins both rename the id → last writer wins, where "last" is the `new/pre/` run-parts order (§7: field conflicts resolve in the filesystem; no arbitration, no namespacing).
- Plugin assigns an invalid id (collides with an existing ball, or not filename-safe per bl-f3e0) → abort.
- No plugin claims an id and no `id_scheme` can produce one → error.
- Plugin assigns an id but a `new/post/` plugin fails → §8 rollback (reverse order) then discard the worktree; the never-committed id frees naturally.
title: Topic: bl new walkthrough (plugin-assigned id)
